dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6281 IH: Community Health Access Through Resilient Grid Energy Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Smith of Washington (for himself, Mr. Valadao, Mrs. King-Hinds, Mr. Moylan, and Mr. Case) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a grant program to facilitate the use of solar energy systems and energy storage technologies at Federally qualified health centers, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Community Health Access Through Resilient Grid Energy Act of 2025 or the CHARGE Act of 2025.

section.header: Grant program for solar energy systems and energy storage technologies at Federally qualified health centers

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary, acting through the Assistant Secretary for Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy of the Department of Energy, shall establish a program to award grants to eligible entities for the conduct of qualifying projects (in this section referred to as the program).

paragraph.text: The Secretary may award a grant under the program if an applicant submits to the Secretary an application at such time, in such form, and containing such information as the Secretary determines appropriate, including a description of—

subparagraph.text: the qualifying project proposed to be conducted by the eligible entity using such grant amounts; and
as applicable, how the eligible entity plans to select any Federally qualified health center with respect to which such qualifying project will be conducted.

paragraph.text: Grant amounts awarded under the program may only be used to conduct a qualifying project.

subsection.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to implement the program $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2030.

paragraph.text: The term applicant means an eligible entity that applies for a grant under the program.

subparagraph.text: a unit of State or local government;
a nonprofit membership organization that operates at the national, regional, or State level and includes within its membership one or more Federally qualified health centers; or
a provider consortia or network that is majority owned or majority controlled by one or more Federally qualified health centers.

paragraph.text: The term energy storage technology has the meaning given that term in section 48E(c)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.
The term Federally qualified health center has the meaning given that term in section 1905(l)(2)(B) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396d(l)(2)(B)).
The term Secretary means the Secretary of Energy.

subparagraph.text: means an engineered assembly of components that capture solar radiation and convert such radiation into an energy source through technologies such as photovoltaic panels or solar thermal collectors; and
includes any supporting infrastructure for the delivery of such energy for use.

paragraph.text: The term qualifying project means a project—

subparagraph.text: to install at one or more Federally qualified health centers a solar energy system or an energy storage technology; or
to provide to one or more Federally qualified health centers technical assistance relating to the design, installation, operation, or use of a solar energy system or energy storage technology.
